assumptions: Detected controls are evidence that relevant code exists, not proof that the control is correct or complete.
Public exposure is inferred from supported route and workflow conventions and must be confirmed against deployment configuration.

AST analysis is handler-scoped for supported TypeScript and JavaScript entry points; same-file helpers and supported Next.js middleware are followed, while arbitrary imported helper behavior remains partially unknown.
Repository evidence coverage: 14/14 candidate files and 73560 bytes analyzed.
unknowns: Sensitive assets were not confirmed in .hedge/context.yml.
Internet-facing deployment surfaces were not confirmed in .hedge/context.yml.
Authentication mechanisms were inferred from code and not confirmed in .hedge/context.yml.
Privileged roles were not confirmed in .hedge/context.yml.
Trusted external services were not confirmed in .hedge/context.yml.

findings.title: Publicly triggerable workflow adds repository write permissions
findings.trustBoundary: Untrusted GitHub event to repository write token
findings.precondition: The workflow token receives write permission on a publicly triggerable event.
findings.potentialImpact: Untrusted event data or a compromised action step may modify repository resources.
missingControls: Separate untrusted analysis from privileged publication
findings.securityInvariant: Publicly triggerable jobs must remain read-only unless a separate trusted approval gate authorizes a narrowly scoped write.

suggestedTest.title: Regression witness for Workflow Hedge Codex remediation (issue_comment)
suggestedTest.purpose: Publicly triggerable jobs must remain read-only unless a separate trusted approval gate authorizes a narrowly scoped write.

findings.remediationPrompt:
Remediate this evidence-backed Hedge finding: Publicly triggerable workflow adds repository write permissions.
Security invariant: Publicly triggerable jobs must remain read-only unless a separate trusted approval gate authorizes a narrowly scoped write.
Entry point: Workflow Hedge Codex remediation (issue_comment)
Missing controls: Least-privilege job permissions, Separate untrusted analysis from privileged publication.
Make the smallest focused change, add a regression witness that fails on the vulnerable revision and passes after the fix, preserve legitimate behavior, run the relevant tests, and explain residual uncertainty.

findings.title: pull_request_target workflow newly combines untrusted PR context with secrets
findings.attackerCapability: Open or modify a pull request from an untrusted branch
findings.trustBoundary: Untrusted pull request metadata to privileged workflow
findings.precondition: The workflow consumes PR-controlled data or checks out PR code while secrets are available.
findings.potentialImpact: Untrusted contribution content may influence a secret-bearing workflow.
missingControls: Strict separation between untrusted PR data and privileged jobs
No execution of PR-controlled code in the secret-bearing job
findings.securityInvariant: A pull_request_target workflow must never execute or interpolate untrusted PR content in a privileged context.

suggestedTest.title: Regression witness for Workflow Hedge security diff (pull_request_target)
suggestedTest.purpose: A pull_request_target workflow must never execute or interpolate untrusted PR content in a privileged context.

findings.remediationPrompt:
Remediate this evidence-backed Hedge finding: pull_request_target workflow newly combines untrusted PR context with secrets.
Security invariant: A pull_request_target workflow must never execute or interpolate untrusted PR content in a privileged context.
Entry point: Workflow Hedge security diff (pull_request_target)
Missing controls: Strict separation between untrusted PR data and privileged jobs, No execution of PR-controlled code in the secret-bearing job.
Make the smallest focused change, add a regression witness that fails on the vulnerable revision and passes after the fix, preserve legitimate behavior, run the relevant tests, and explain residual uncertainty.

findings.title: Privileged workflow checks out untrusted pull request code
findings.attackerCapability: Modify code on the pull request head branch
findings.trustBoundary: Untrusted pull request code to privileged runner
findings.precondition: The job checks out the PR head while secrets or write permissions are available.
findings.potentialImpact: Repository-controlled scripts or build steps may execute with credentials available to the job.
missingControls: Never execute PR head code in the privileged job
Artifact-only handoff to a separate credential-free verification job
findings.securityInvariant: A secret-bearing or write-capable workflow must not execute code from an untrusted pull request revision.

suggestedTest.title: Regression witness for Workflow Hedge security diff (pull_request_target)
suggestedTest.purpose: A secret-bearing or write-capable workflow must not execute code from an untrusted pull request revision.

findings.remediationPrompt:
Remediate this evidence-backed Hedge finding: Privileged workflow checks out untrusted pull request code.
Security invariant: A secret-bearing or write-capable workflow must not execute code from an untrusted pull request revision.
Entry point: Workflow Hedge security diff (pull_request_target)
Missing controls: Never execute PR head code in the privileged job, Artifact-only handoff to a separate credential-free verification job.
Make the smallest focused change, add a regression witness that fails on the vulnerable revision and passes after the fix, preserve legitimate behavior, run the relevant tests, and explain residual uncertainty.

findings.title: Public entry point newly depends on a privileged secret
findings.attackerCapability: Repeatedly invoke the public route and influence error paths or outbound requests
findings.precondition: The route consumes the secret during request processing.
findings.potentialImpact: Error handling, logging, or request construction could expose or misuse the credential.
findings.securityInvariant: A public request must never control, reveal, or redirect use of a privileged credential.

suggestedTest.title: Regression witness for Workflow Hedge security diff (pull_request_target)
suggestedTest.purpose: A public request must never control, reveal, or redirect use of a privileged credential.
